title: Copper Futures Hit 3-Month High as Data Center Demand Outlook Improves
description: Copper Futures Hit 3-Month High as Data Center Demand Outlook Improves Reuters
htmlContent:
Copper Futures Hit 3-Month High as Data Center Demand Outlook Improves

By Reuters Commodities Desk • June 4, 2026

LONDON, June 4 (Reuters) – Copper futures climbed to their highest level in three months on Wednesday, buoyed by an improving outlook for demand from the technology sector, with accelerating AI data center construction identified as a primary driver by analysts.

LME three-month copper rose 1.4% to $10,820 per tonne in morning trading, the highest since early March, following a string of high-profile announcements from US technology giants about expanded cloud and AI compute infrastructure.

"Every hyperscale facility being built right now requires hundreds of tonnes of copper – in wiring, busbars, transformers, and cooling systems," said James Whitfield, head of base metals research at Macquarie Group. "The pipeline of projects is enormous and growing faster than supply can respond."

Traders cited fresh capital expenditure commitments from Microsoft, Google and Amazon as direct catalysts for Wednesday's move, with each company announcing multi-billion dollar data centre expansions in the United States, Germany and Singapore during the past week.

Supply-side risks compounded the bullish sentiment. Chilean copper output has run below seasonal averages for four consecutive months, following prolonged labour negotiations at Escondida and Collahuasi, the country's two largest mines. Combined, those operations account for roughly 8% of global copper supply.

Inventories on the LME fell for the twelfth straight session to stand at 87,250 tonnes, their lowest since November 2024, while COMEX warehouse stocks have also declined sharply in recent weeks.

The move higher came despite broader commodity markets trading mixed, with oil prices slipping and gold holding near recent highs. Analysts said copper's outperformance reflected its unique exposure to the structural demand theme of electrification and digital infrastructure.

Goldman Sachs this week raised its 12-month copper price target, and Morgan Stanley noted in a client memo that the metal's fundamentals had "rarely looked more compelling on a multi-year horizon."

Reporting by Sarah Jennings and Marcus Chow; Editing by David Gregorio

Tags: copper, futures, data center, AI, demand, LME, commodities

Sentiment signal: bullish
